message:
fix(share): stop swallowing serde errors; reject symlinked signing paths; fail hard on chmod errors

Audits every `serde_json::to_string[_pretty](...).unwrap_or_else(...)`
in the share crate and converts it to proper error propagation via two
new helpers. Also hardens the signing and deploy paths against unsafe
inputs that were previously silently ignored.

New helpers in `mcp-agent-mail-share/src/lib.rs`
------------------------------------------------
* `pub(crate) fn encode_json_pretty<T: Serialize>(value, context) -> ShareResult<String>`
* `pub(crate) fn encode_json<T: Serialize>(value, context) -> ShareResult<String>`

Both map serialization failures to `ShareError::ManifestParse { message }`
with the caller-supplied context prefix, so downstream errors retain
which payload failed and why instead of turning into an empty `{}` or
`[]` default.

A new unit test, `encode_json_pretty_surfaces_serialization_failure`,
uses a hand-rolled `FailingSerialize` stub that always returns
`serde::ser::Error::custom("boom")` to assert that both helpers
surface the error, tag it as `ShareError::ManifestParse`, and preserve
the context prefix in the error message.

Call-site migration (no behaviour change on the happy path, but errors
are now observable)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
* `bundle.rs`:
    - `bundle_attachments` — attachment manifest serialization failures
      now propagate instead of writing `"[]"` on top of real data.
    - `maybe_chunk_database` — chunk config JSON is now required to
      serialise; previously a failure wrote an empty file that the
      deploy tooling could not parse.
    - `write_bundle_scaffolding` — bundle `manifest.json` serialization
      failures propagate instead of falling back to an empty string,
      which would have produced an unsigned/invalid manifest.
    - `export_viewer_data` — messages.json and meta.json now require
      successful serialization.
* `scrub.rs` — sanitised attachment writeback in `scrub_snapshot` uses
  `encode_json` so a broken payload aborts the scrub instead of
  overwriting the message row with `"[]"`.
* `static_render.rs` — sitemap.json, search_index.json, navigation.json,
  and redaction_audit.json all propagate serialization failures.
* `deploy.rs` — `record_deploy` switches to `encode_json_pretty` (was
  already returning an error, now goes through the shared helper);
  `write_deploy_tooling` emits `deploy_report.json` via
  `encode_json_pretty` instead of defaulting to `"{}"` on failure.

Signature shape change in `prompt.rs`
-------------------------------------
* `format_json_output(&outcome, executed, error) -> String` becomes
  `-> Result<String, WizardError>`. A serialization failure now yields
  `WizardError::new(WizardErrorCode::InternalError, ...)` with the
  underlying error chained into the message.
* The wizard test (`mod tests::...`) now calls `.expect("wizard output
  should encode")` on the result.
* `mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs::run_native_wizard` picks up the new
  `Result` shape and maps it into `CliError::Other` alongside the
  existing `serde_json::from_str` error branch.

Symlink hardening in `crypto.rs::sign_manifest`
-----------------------------------------------
* New early checks:
    - `require_real_crypto_file(signing_key_path, "signing key")` —
      rejects a signing key that is actually a symlink (`ErrorKind::InvalidInput`,
      message contains "must not be a symlink").
    - `ensure_real_crypto_directory(parent)` on the signature output's
      parent directory — rejects a symlinked parent for the same
      reason.
* Two new `#[cfg(unix)]` tests:
    - `sign_rejects_symlinked_signing_key` — creates a real key, a
      symlink pointing at it, runs `sign_manifest` through the symlink,
      and asserts the rejection shape.
    - `sign_rejects_symlinked_signature_parent_directory` — creates a
      real "outside" directory, a symlink pointing at it, runs
      `sign_manifest` with an output path inside the symlinked parent,
      and asserts the same rejection.

Chmod-failure in `deploy.rs::write_deploy_tooling`
--------------------------------------------------
* Previously, the Unix-only `set_permissions(...0o755)` call was
  prefixed with `let _ =`, so a chmod failure silently left the
  generated `scripts/validate_deploy.sh` without its execute bit and
  the deploy would fail later with a confusing "permission denied"
  from the shell.
* Now the call propagates via `.map_err(ShareError::Io)?` and the
  `deploy_tooling_contains_expected_files` test asserts the resulting
  file mode is `0o755`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
